commit.message:
feat(master): add explicit deactivation lifecycle

Summary:
- add `deactivate/0` and `deactivate(:preop)` plus a public
  `:deactivated` state
- track the desired runtime target in the master and make
  activation-blocked and recovery flows settle to OP, SAFEOP, or PREOP
- add a dedicated deactivation helper and update docs and tests for the
  new lifecycle semantics

Rationale:
- separate leaving OP from tearing the session down so callers can keep
  a live session while outputs are off
- stop reconnect and recovery paths from auto-promoting slaves back to
  OP when the runtime was intentionally deactivated

Validation:
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(simulator): add command-targeted WKC skew

Summary:
- add `{:command_wkc_offset, command_name, delta}` as a queued or sticky
  simulator fault and route it through per-datagram WKC adjustment
- add simulator visibility for active command WKC offsets and cover the new
  fault with unit tests plus integration scenario 14
- refresh simulator docs and scenario notes so the command-targeted WKC path
  is part of the public fault model and scenario loop

Rationale:
- targeted logical WKC skew covered LRW contribution faults, but the simulator
  still could not model poll-specific or command-specific WKC degradation
- health-poll failures with a healthy LRW domain are a real class of master
  recovery path and need direct regression coverage
- the simulator docs should describe the currently supported fault shapes and
  keep the scenario backlog focused on the remaining mailbox timing gap

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/simulator_test.exs test/integration/simulator/14_command_targeted_wkc_mismatch_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator --max-failures 1
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(simulator): add targeted WKC and startup mailbox scenarios

Summary:
- add targeted logical WKC skew fault injection and route it only through
  logical datagram contributions
- add startup mailbox-abort coverage with a configured mailbox test driver
  plus new simulator scenarios 12 and 13
- refresh simulator docs and harden the integration harness so the standalone
  simulator suite is stable with the broader scenario set

Rationale:
- global WKC skew was too coarse to model one slave disappearing from the
  logical PDO contribution while fixed-address health polling stayed honest
- startup mailbox failures are a real PREOP activation path and needed direct
  simulator coverage, not just public SDO abort tests
- the simulator README and scenario notes should track the current fault API
  and scenario loop, and the test harness needed less aggressive defaults to
  avoid false startup flakes

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/simulator_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator/12_startup_mailbox_abort_test.exs test/integration/simulator/13_targeted_wkc_mismatch_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator --max-failures 1
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(simulator): add milestone-aware fault scripts

Summary:
- replace the old exchange-only script wrapper with sequential
  `{:fault_script, steps}` handling in the simulator runtime
- allow scripts to pause on `{:wait_for_milestone, ...}` and resume into
  later exchange or slave-local fault steps
- add scenario 11 and update simulator docs and existing script-based
  tests to use the new abstraction

Rationale:
- `exchange_script` stopped matching the real API once scripts needed to
  mix waits and non-exchange faults
- moving to a single sequential script runner keeps fault timing inside
  the simulator instead of pushing brittle choreography into tests
- the extra snapshot visibility makes queued and waiting script state
  observable enough for integration assertions

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/simulator_test.exs test/integration/simulator/07_combined_fault_script_test.exs test/integration/simulator/08_delayed_slave_fault_script_test.exs test/integration/simulator/09_milestone_fault_script_test.exs test/integration/simulator/11_embedded_milestone_fault_script_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator --max-failures 1
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(simulator): add milestone and segmented mailbox faults

Add milestone-triggered fault scheduling and segmented mailbox abort injection to the simulator runtime. This also fixes segmented upload response handling and adds dedicated integration coverage for milestone-aware scripts and segmented SDO aborts.

Validation: mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(runtime): add delayed simulator faults and limits

Summary:
- add delayed simulator fault scheduling with `{:after_ms, delay_ms,
  fault}` and expose `scheduled_faults` in simulator snapshots
- add the new `08` delayed slave-fault integration scenario and update
  simulator docs to reflect the expanded fault API
- reject impossible master topology/address combinations and stabilize
  the PREOP mailbox abort scenario with a less brittle timeout budget

Rationale:
- mixed recovery scenarios need delayed slave-local mutations without
  relying on brittle sleeps or oversized exchange scripts
- address-space limits should fail explicitly before the runtime tries
  to encode invalid auto-increment or station addresses
- the mailbox abort test is about CoE abort behavior, not transport
  timeouts during its initial PREOP roundtrip

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/simulator_test.exs
  test/integration/simulator/08_delayed_slave_fault_script_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Teach scan_dirs to scan directories recursively (#347)

Scan listed subdirectories recursively for nested source files.

AI-assisted using Codex for implementation and testing.

Resolves #226

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
docs: Clarify scan_dirs behavior (#345)

Clarify in both the tutorial and reference manual that
autodoc.scan_dirs controls scanning for AutoDoc source comments
and .autodoc files, while gapdoc.scan_dirs only controls scanning
for GAPDoc comments. This also corrects the tutorial examples to
use the autodoc record for the documented behavior.

Fixes #263

AI assistance was provided by Codex for reviewing the existing
documentation, drafting the wording changes, and updating the
generated manual fixtures.

commit.message:
refactor(simulator): split driver adapters and script faults

Summary:
- move simulator authoring out of real slave drivers into optional
  `MyDriver.Simulator` adapters and rename `process_data_model` to
  `signal_model`
- add queued runtime and UDP fault scripts with visibility through
  `next_fault` and `pending_faults`
- add simulator scenario docs and integration coverage for timeout, WKC,
  disconnect, corruption, mailbox abort, safeop retreat, and combined
  scripts

Rationale:
- keep real drivers focused on runtime-facing identity and signal modeling
  instead of returning simulator-owned structs
- make simulator recovery scenarios deterministic without sticky
  inject-and-clear choreography in the tests
- document the simulator fault boundary so runtime faults and raw UDP
  corruption stay separate and spec-shaped

Validation:
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(web-ui): prove inline markdown styling behavior

Replace the remaining inline-code source-shape check with runtime coverage by making inline code styling explicit in both static and finalized streaming markdown output.

Task: @01KKB5HYXXE1DH0T13T6BK2SYC
Spec: @streaming-markdown-component
Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Prefix skill invocation names with docs- without breaking update flow.

Rename skill frontmatter names to docs-* and bump patch versions across the catalog, remove directory-name coupling from validation, and update installer update logic so existing installs update seamlessly even when name values change.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(release): 发布0.33.1并修复WebUI内嵌启动链路

- 将 webui_runtime 的 API/WebUI 启动从外部 shell 子进程切换为插件内嵌 uvicorn 托管，补齐关键路径可观测日志。
- 新增 infrastructure/webui_runtime_server.py：统一管理运行时启动/停止，WebUI 直接托管 data/static/webui 并内置 /api 反向代理。
- 删除已废弃生产脚本 scripts/run_api_prod.sh 与 scripts/run_webui_prod.sh，并同步 README 与运行时端口契约测试。
- 增加构造阶段 runtime bootstrap 与后台任务取消逻辑，降低 enable_webui 打开后的生命周期漏触发风险。
- 更新版本声明到 0.33.1（main.py、metadata.yaml）并补充 CHANGELOG。
- 回归：bash scripts/run_regression.sh（537 passed）。

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
refactor(simulator): use a struct for runtime state

Summary:
- add `EtherCAT.Simulator.State` as the internal runtime state
  struct
- update the simulator GenServer to initialize and update typed
  state instead of ad hoc maps
- move fault read-model conversion into snapshot rendering so the
  runtime state shape stays consistent

Rationale:
- the simulator state shape is fixed and should use a struct for
  stronger invariants and clearer internal ownership
- keeping runtime state typed avoids accidental loss of the struct
  when applying updates during fault clearing and snapshot reads

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/ethercat/simulator_test.exs
  test/integration/simulator/ring_test.exs --max-failures 1

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(worktree): migrate daemon identity and cwd

Move daemon-facing worktree call sites to project-root identity,
validate dispatch cwd ownership, and cover the worktree dispatch
contract with CLI and daemon regressions.

Task: @01KKCEE31YMBRNYTF7G1VVR9GH
Spec: @worktree-support
Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(simulator): simplify runtime to singleton

Summary:
- route simulator and UDP lifecycle through a single named runtime
  and supervisor
- remove unused per-server simulator and slave API variants and
  update docs and ring integration coverage to match
- add simulator lifecycle regression tests for start and stop
  cleanup paths

Rationale:
- the codebase uses a single simulator instance, so carrying
  multi-runtime plumbing added shutdown bugs and unnecessary API
  complexity
- a singleton runtime makes UDP metadata, signal wiring, and test
  cleanup deterministic for integration runs

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/ethercat/simulator_test.exs
  test/ethercat/simulator/slave/logical_test.exs
  test/ethercat/simulator/slave/process_image_test.exs
  test/ethercat/simulator/slave/al_test.exs
  test/ethercat/simulator/slave/esc_image_test.exs --max-failures 1
- timeout 120s mix test test/integration/simulator/ring_test.exs
  --max-failures 1

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Upgrade BotKit to Fedify 2.0

Align BotKit with Fedify 2.0.3 and its modular package split.
Move vocab and runtime imports to @fedify/vocab,
@fedify/vocab-runtime, and @fedify/denokv.

Update the public API to match Fedify 2.0 by replacing
LanguageTag-based surfaces with Intl.Locale, using plain strings
for software.version, and removing parseSemVer(), SemVer,
LanguageTag, and parseLanguageTag() exports.

Refresh examples, docs, and CHANGES.md to reflect the new
package paths and API changes. Update lockfiles and manifests
for the new dependency graph.

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
let CLI.run own process exit

Treat the CLI entry routine as a true command entrypoint: help and version exit directly, parse failures exit 1, and normal completion falls through with implicit success.

Update exe/jrf to call Jrf::CLI.run without wrapping it in exit, which removes the need for the local parser exit sentinel.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: jrf
repository.description: JSON transformer with the power and speed of Ruby

commit.message:
inline the remaining OptionParser logic

Keep the parser setup linear inside CLI.run by using an explicit sentinel for help/version exits and validating --atomic-write-bytes inline instead of extracting a helper.

This leaves the fixed help text intact while matching the simpler structure requested in review.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: jrf
repository.description: JSON transformer with the power and speed of Ruby

commit.message:
refactor(simulator): streamline runtime and ring integration

Summary:
- add a simpler simulator lifecycle with `start/1` and unified
  `stop/1` handling for in-memory and UDP-backed runtimes
- replace the old simulator deep test with ring-shaped integration tests
  for both simulator and real hardware using EK1100, EL1809, and EL2809
- narrow the public simulator slave API toward driver-backed devices and
  align docs/tests with the new ring and hardware-first model

Rationale:
- the previous simulator setup exposed too much internal structure and
  required awkward manual endpoint cleanup in tests
- ring-shaped integration coverage better matches the actual target
  topology and keeps simulator and hardware validation aligned
- driver-backed simulation is the clearer long-term public API than
  profile-style convenience builders

Validation:
- mix test
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

simplify the OptionParser wiring

Restore the fixed help text output, keep the option definitions inline in CLI.run, and avoid routing parser results through callback lambdas.

This preserves the CLI behavior from the earlier PR while matching the simpler structure requested in review.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
switch the CLI to OptionParser

Replace the ad hoc option loop with Ruby stdlib OptionParser, preserving front-of-command option parsing while adding conventional -V/--version support alongside -v/--verbose.

Keep the existing help content shape, retain --no-jit and atomic-write parsing, and add executable-level coverage for help/version behavior.

add a --no-jit CLI option

Enable YJIT after option parsing by default, but allow callers to opt out with --no-jit when they want deterministic non-JIT startup.

Update help text and add executable-level tests covering default enablement and the opt-out path.

commit.message:
refactor(simulator): group runtime internals

Summary:
- Move simulator runtime helpers under `EtherCAT.Simulator.Runtime`.
- Move slave device and mailbox internals under
  `EtherCAT.Simulator.Slave.Runtime`.
- Update docs and tests to the new internal module boundaries.

Rationale:
- Keep `lib/ethercat/simulator/` and `lib/ethercat/simulator/slave/`
  focused on public/authored concepts instead of implementation details.
- Remove the confusing top-level `Device`/`Mailbox` surface and make the
  runtime split easier to audit and extend.

Validation:
- mix format
- mix test
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(simulator): generalize digital card hydration

Summary:
- generalize the digital_io simulator profile for image and channel modes
- add realistic simulator hydration for the EL1809 and EL2809
- extend driver tests to cover channel-based Beckhoff card definitions

Rationale:
- the simulator should model real digital input and output cards, not
  only a byte-wide mirrored demo device
- example integration drivers are more useful when they can hydrate
  realistic simulator devices with correct identity and signal layout
- keeping the old image mode preserves the existing simulator tests while
  enabling more accurate channel-oriented devices

Validation:
- mix format
- mix test test/ethercat/slave/driver_test.exs
- mix test
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
